MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matthew Woodbury.

Ms. Alavi requested a 45-day continuance stating that they were having complications with the Viacom shares (only asset in the Trust). The matter was continued to January 18, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order To Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision, To Appoint Successor Trustee And To Fix Bond
Off Calendar Jul-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Woodbury (Petitioner), Avid Alavi (Attorney for Petitioner).

Ms. Alavi submitted the Order, and the Court granted the Order bringing the trust under court supervision and setting the bond at $135,000.00 (signing the Order same in open court). Inventory and Appraisal must be filed by November 7, 2007, and the accounting must be filed no later than two weeks before October 7, 2008.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Dave Davenport CSR# 1694; Court Investigator: John Cushner; Bailiff: D. Handa; Clerk: H. Yung.

Petition For Order To Bring Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision, To Appoint Successor Trustee And To Fix Bond
Off Calendar Jun-14-2007 Continued To Jul-26-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Woodbury (Petitioner); Evita Alavi (Attorney for Mr. Woodbury); Mary O'Keefe (Court-appointed Attorney for Ms. Hsu). Nancy Hsu (Trustor) was unwilling to appear and was excused.

The Court continued this matter to July 26, 2007, because notice must be sent to the beneficiary, First Chinese Baptist Church, and a copy of the trust needs to be submitted to the Court.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Dave Davenport CSR# 1694; Court Investigator: Cynthia Jones; Bailiff: M. Anderson; Clerk: H. Yung.
